Plaintiffs' motion for order maintaining current trial date with preference.

APPEARANCES: Andrew Chew, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLP 415/898-1555 for pltfs & moving parties; E. Jacob Lubarsky, Esq. of McKenna Long & Aldridge LLP 415/267-4105 for deft Certainteed Corporation; Heather J. Zacharia, Esq. of Walsworth Franklin Bevins & McCall, LLP 415/781-7072 for deft RV Cloud Company, J-M Manufacturing Company, Inc. & Westburne Supply, Inc.; Lauren N. Peebles, Esq. of Berry & Berry 510/250-0430 Designated Defense Counsel.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Plaintiffs' motion for order maintaining current trial date with preference is denied without prejudice. Plaintiffs failed to demonstrate that Mr. Miller's health is such that preference is necessary to prevent prejudicing his interest in the litigation, as required by C.C.P. 36(a). The Court however will treat this as an expedited trial and if a courtroom is available and there are no preference cases ahead of it, the Court will do its best to ensure a courtroom. Order signed in open court. (The Court advised parties that if a courtroom becomes available the week of 12/08/14, it will assign this case out. The return holding trial date is currently 12/15/14.) Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tara L. Williams, CSR #13428 of Aiken Welch 510/451-1580 (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment, Or Alternatively, Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-13-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Vladislav V. Luskin of WALSWORTH FRANKLIN BEVINS & McCALL, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing for defendant / moving party Fraser's Boiler Service, Inc.

RULING:
DEFENDANT WESTBURNE SUPPLY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR ALTERNATIVELY, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Defendant submitted to Court's tentative. Tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Westburne Supply, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Miller was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because it did not demonstrate that plaintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive interrogatories designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiffs have to support their contention of liability against defendant. Defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is off calendar as moot as it is undisputed that plaintiffs are not making a claim for punitive damages against defendant. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)
